verse_specific_anchors: the wardrobe simile structure — wearer-unchanging-while-clothes-change is preserved as customer-requirement-unchanging-while-implementations-change

the 'jīrṇāni' (worn-out) modifier preserved — implementations are not just changed but specifically retired (Perl→Python, REST→gRPC, monolith→microservices→serverless), each cycle described as wear-out
Ramanuja's affect-reframing (junior grieves the discarded cloth, senior receives wardrobe changes as ordinary) is 2.22-specific because the simile is the affect-reframing device
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 2.13 (childhood→youth→old-age body change — overlaps but lacks the clothes simile), BG 2.20 (the unborn/undying soul — overlaps the dehī's continuity but lacks the active 'casting off + taking up' simile), BG 15.7-8 (the embodied self carries the senses from one body to another — overlaps but is in a different chapter and frames the transition as the Lord's manifestation rather than as ordinary wardrobe-change). None of these have the wardrobe simile's structure plus the affect-reframing. The analog is 2.22-specific. PASS — not transferable.
